name: Isla Mayor and the Doñana channels — return through the Guadalquivir Marshes
name_original: Isla Mayor y los canales de Doñana — vuelta por las Marismas del Guadalquivir
region_intro: Hiking trails around Isla Mayor traverse a landscape dominated by the vast Guadalquivir marshes and extensive rice fields, which are the largest in Europe. The terrain is predominantly flat, characterized by wetlands and plains, especially when the rice fields are flooded from June to November. Hikers can also find pine forests and wild olive groves within areas like the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve, forming an integral part of the Doñana Marshes ecosystem. Best hiking trails around Isla Mayor The most popular hiking route is Pinares de Aznalcázar Loop, a 6.9 miles (11.0 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route winds through pine forests and wild olive groves within the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve. Another top favourite among local hikers is Walk through the Arrayanes Ravine - circular through the Birds Ravine, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This circular route offers varied ravine scenery and takes approximately 2 hours 35 minutes. Local hikers also love the Dehesa de Abajo Loop, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 7 minutes. This route is known for its birdwatching opportunities. Hiking around Isla Mayor is defined by expansive rice fields, wetlands, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 5 easy and 10 moderate routes. The routes in Isla Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from around 50 reviews. More than 180 hikers have used komoot to explore Isla Mayor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Isla Mayor?
A: Isla Mayor offers a diverse network of &lt;strong&gt;15 hiking routes&lt;/strong&gt;. These include 5 easy trails and 10 moderate options, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Isla Mayor?
A: The terrain in Isla Mayor is predominantly flat, characterized by vast Guadalquivir marshes and extensive rice fields, which are the largest in Europe. You'll also find areas with pine forests and wild olive groves, particularly within the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve. The landscape is defined by wetlands and plains, especially when the rice fields are flooded from June to November.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families in Isla Mayor?
A: Yes, Isla Mayor has &lt;strong&gt;5 easy hiking routes&lt;/strong&gt; perfect for beginners or families. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14593394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Dehesa de Abajo Viewpoint loop from Los Montes&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.66 km long and offers beautiful views without significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Isla Mayor?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Isla Mayor are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005951184/pinares-de-aznalcazar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinares de Aznalcázar Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through pine forests, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005931700/dehesa-de-abajo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Abajo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, known for its birdwatching opportunities.

Q: What are the best opportunities for birdwatching while hiking in Isla Mayor?
A: Isla Mayor is a prime destination for birdwatching, especially in the rice fields and the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve. The flooded rice paddies attract over a hundred bird species, including White Storks, Greater Flamingos, and Glossy Ibises. The Dehesa de Abajo is particularly renowned for its diverse birdlife, hosting species like the black coot and the endangered marbled duck. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Dehesa de Abajo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for excellent viewing spots.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to see along the trails?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Abajo Visitor Center&lt;/a&gt; is a great starting point. Hikers can also discover unique architectural elements in Isla Mayor itself, such as 'gañanías' (former laborers' lodgings) and 'las casitas de los ingleses' (houses of early British settlers), reflecting the region's history with rice farming.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Isla Mayor?
A: The best time to hike in Isla Mayor depends on your interests. For birdwatching, the flooded rice fields from June to November offer continuous food sources for a vast array of species. Generally, spring and autumn provide milder temperatures, making them ideal for comfortable hiking. Summer can be hot, but the wetlands are at their most vibrant.

Q: Is Isla Mayor suitable for dog-friendly hikes?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary, the open and natural landscapes of Isla Mayor, particularly in areas like the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve, often welcome well-behaved dogs on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage or park guidelines for the most current information on specific routes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Isla Mayor?
A: The hiking routes in Isla Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.3 stars from around 50 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Hikers frequently praise the unique wetland scenery, the abundance of birdlife, and the peaceful, flat terrain that makes for enjoyable walks.

Q: Are there any longer hiking options for experienced hikers in Isla Mayor?
A: For those seeking a longer trek, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006534590/isla-mayor-and-the-donana-channels-return-through-the-guadalquivir-marshes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Mayor and the Doñana channels — return through the Guadalquivir Marshes&lt;/a&gt; route is an excellent choice. This moderate trail spans 18.3 km, offering an extended exploration of the region's unique landscapes and marshes with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Where can I park when visiting hiking trails in Isla Mayor?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes within or near the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve, designated parking areas are typically available close to visitor centers or popular access points. In Isla Mayor town, street parking is generally available. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for detailed parking information.

Q: What makes hiking in Isla Mayor unique compared to other regions?
A: Hiking in Isla Mayor offers a truly unique experience due to its distinctive landscape. It's dominated by Europe's largest rice fields and vast Guadalquivir marshes, forming a crucial part of the Doñana Marshes ecosystem. This creates an unparalleled wetland environment, rich in biodiversity and birdlife, offering a different kind of natural beauty compared to mountainous or forested regions.
tips: Agricultural fields located on the outskirts of the town of Isla Mayor, in the direction of the center of the Doñana National Park. Follow the path that follows the Main Channel of Isla Mínima.
Town that is located in the marsh plain of the Guadalquivir river. It is located very close to the Doñana natural park and surrounded by compos and humid spaces full of biodiversity.
